learning_outcomes.description: Engage with past and present music forms and analyse objectively the complex nature of both contemporary music and the music industry.

Critically examine popular music texts within contexts not restricted to, but still mindful of, more common techniques of subjective aesthetic evaluation, such as industry, postmodernism, globalisation, technology, and culture.
Partake in informed written and verbal debates concerning the political economy of cultural production.
Demonstrate a keen knowledge of musical production which takes place outside of mainstream contexts.
Achieve a greater understanding of how issues such as race, cultural identity, gender and politics impact on both cultural and commercial production.

assessments.due_date:
Week 5 test 25/08/2026

Week 9 test 22/09/2026

Week 13 test 27/10/2026

The marks from your two best attempts out of the three tests will count towards your final mark. This means that a student can miss one test and still achieve the maximum possible marks for the In-Class Online Multiple-Choice Tests assessment component (30%). You must be present from the commencement of your tutorial during test weeks. Late arrivals without a valid explanation will not be permitted to sit the test.
assessments.task_description:
In-class online tests, held during tutorials in weeks 5, 9 and 13.

Each test consists of 30 multiple-choice questions based on the course readings: the week 5 test (Tue 25/8) covers weeks 2, 3, 4 & 5; the week 9 test (Tue 22/9) covers weeks 6, 7, 8 & 9; the week 13 test (Tue 27/10) covers weeks 10, 11 & 13, plus 10 course revision questions.

You will be permitted to consult any notes you have made from the weekly readings, but no other documents or web pages may be viewed on devices during the test.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submitted in class via Blackboard Assessment section.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
At the end of week 6 (5pm Friday September 4), you are required to submit a 500-word proposal outlining your plans for the final essay (see Assessment item 4 before you begin). The proposal must be accompanied by a 500-word annotated bibliography presenting examples of initial research you have conducted.


In the form of a brief essay, the proposal should outline:

·      The broader topic or topics (e.g., gender, race, industry, technology, etc).

·      The aspect of music to be analysed within that context

·      An indication of the indented argument

·      Further potential research to be undertaken

·      Possible musical examples to be used

 

The bibliography should include:

·      Five scholarly/academic sources listed in Chicago author/date style

·      Annotations (approximately 100 words) summarising each source and indicating its intended role in your argument


See the criteria sheet in the Assessment section at Learn.UQ for more details.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Present a 2000-word research essay examining a post-1975 popular music text—e.g. album (vinyl, CD, mp3 or streaming release) or band/performer or music video collection—within one or more of the following contexts:

  • gender/sexuality/identity
  • race/ethnicity/nation
  • industry/economy
  • technology/new media
  • culture/society/politics
  • musicology/genre
  • postmodernism/hauntology


See full criteria sheet at the Blackboard Assessment section. Initial preparation for this task must be untaken for the Research Proposal/Annotated Bibliography (Assessment 1)
assessments.submission_guidelines: Essays to be submitted online as a Word document via the Turn It In link in the Assessment section at Learn.UQ by 5pm Friday in week 13.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
